en.extract:
The yellow-winged pytilia (Pytilia hypogrammica), also known as the red-faced pytilia, is an African estrildid finch.


Distribution
The finch has an estimated global extent of occurrence of 250,000 km2 (97,000 sq mi). It is sparsely distributed across West Africa, from Guinea to far north-western Democratic Republic of the Congo.


References


External links
BirdLife International species factsheet

The '''yellow-winged pytilia''' ('''''Pytilia hypogrammica'''''), also known as the '''red-faced pytilia''', is an [[Africa]]n [[estrildid finch]].

==Distribution==
The finch has an estimated global extent of occurrence of {{cvt|250000|km2}}. It is sparsely distributed across [[West Africa]], from [[Guinea]] to far north-western Democratic Republic of the Congo.
